title: Add regression coverage for verify, commit, and post-commit interruption paths
goal: Add regression coverage proving that interrupted verify, commit, and post-commit flows resume deterministically using the canonical continuation contract and do not corrupt lifecycle state.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 redesign treats verify and commit as resumable checkpoints and requires commit to remain non-terminal until explicit terminal completion. This ticket verifies that interruption during those stages does not create duplicate logical completion, replacement transaction identities, or ambiguous continuation behavior.
scope: Add tests for interruption during verify-started but not yet completed flows.
Add tests for interruption after verify failure where the transaction remains resumable.
Add tests for interruption after verify success and before any required commit action completes.
Add tests for interruption during commit-started but not yet completed flows.
Add tests for interruption after successful commit and before explicit terminal completion.
Add tests for repeated resume of already-verified or already-committed states.
Validate that canonical next_action remains the continuation dispatcher across these interrupted states.
Validate that exact active transaction identity is preserved throughout verify and commit resumption.
out_of_scope: Redesigning verify or commit business logic itself.
Broad compatibility testing for malformed persistence artifacts beyond what is needed for these interruption scenarios.
Operator-facing documentation updates outside minimal test descriptions.
inputs: Outputs from p0-t01, p0-t02, and p0-t03
Outputs from p1-t02, p1-t03, p1-t04, p2-t02, p2-t04, p2-t05, and p2-t06
Runtime test suite and any transaction lifecycle test helpers
expected_outputs: Regression tests covering interrupted verify flows.
Regression tests covering interrupted commit flows.
Regression tests covering interrupted post-commit, pre-terminal flows.
Assertions proving deterministic continuation from canonical next_action and exact active transaction identity.
A concise note describing which interruption checkpoints are now covered.

required_test_scenarios.name: interrupted_verify_in_progress
required_test_scenarios.description: A transaction interrupted after verify has started but before verify has resolved resumes the same active transaction and continues from canonical next_action without minting a new tx_id.

required_test_scenarios.name: interrupted_verify_failure_repair_loop
required_test_scenarios.description: A transaction interrupted after verify failure remains non-terminal, preserves exact tx_id, and resumes as further action rather than silently ending or restarting.

required_test_scenarios.name: interrupted_verified_pre_commit
required_test_scenarios.description: A transaction interrupted after verify success but before commit continues according to canonical next_action and does not require heuristic reconstruction.

required_test_scenarios.name: interrupted_commit_in_progress
required_test_scenarios.description: A transaction interrupted after commit start but before commit completion resumes the same active transaction and does not duplicate commit lifecycle behavior.

required_test_scenarios.name: interrupted_post_commit_pre_terminal
required_test_scenarios.description: A transaction interrupted after successful commit but before explicit terminal completion remains non-terminal committed work and resumes toward explicit end rather than being treated as already done.

required_test_scenarios.name: repeated_resume_already_verified
required_test_scenarios.description: Repeated resume of an already-verified transaction does not corrupt state, change tx_id, or bypass canonical next_action.

required_test_scenarios.name: repeated_resume_already_committed
required_test_scenarios.description: Repeated resume of an already-committed transaction does not reopen commit, mint a new transaction, or skip explicit terminal handling.

continuation_invariants_under_test: Resume reuses the exact existing tx_id.
Resume does not mint a replacement transaction identity.
Valid canonical next_action is preferred over status-derived heuristics.
Commit remains non-terminal until explicit terminal completion.
Interrupted verify or commit does not cause duplicate logical completion.
Repeated resume of the same interrupted state does not corrupt lifecycle state.
Active transaction identity remains exact across interruption and continuation.
acceptance_criteria: Regression coverage exists for interrupted verify-started, verify-failed, verified-pre-commit, commit-started, and post-commit pre-terminal states.
Tests demonstrate that the same active transaction is resumed in each interrupted scenario.
Tests demonstrate that canonical next_action drives continuation rather than reconstructed status heuristics.
Tests demonstrate that successful commit does not by itself imply terminal success.
Tests demonstrate that repeated resume of already-verified and already-committed states does not corrupt transaction state or duplicate logical completion.
Tests remain consistent with the minimal canonical continuation contract and the canonical checkpoint event model.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that each interruption test asserts exact tx_id continuity.
Confirm that each interruption test distinguishes committed from done.
Confirm that verify and commit checkpoint state are treated as resumable non-terminal states where the plan requires it.
Confirm that no test depends on phase-style or step-style metadata for correctness.
Confirm that canonical next_action is asserted as part of continuation behavior where applicable.
notes: This ticket focuses on interruption behavior around verify and commit because those checkpoints are the most likely place for lifecycle ambiguity to leak back into the runtime.
Tests should model real resumable states rather than only happy-path full runs.
Where possible, each scenario should validate both machine-readable continuation state and resulting behavioral correctness.
